description_long: This is a single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with a streamlined fuselage and a classic World War II fighter design. The aircraft features a bubble canopy cockpit positioned towards the front of the fuselage, providing wide visibility for the pilot. The wings are straight and low-mounted, with visible ailerons and flaps, and the tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear appears to be a taildragger configuration, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel. The exterior is finished in a metallic gray with some markings and a yellow accent on the tail. The overall construction suggests a metal airframe, likely aluminum, with glass for the cockpit canopy and rubber for the tires.
description: A metallic gray, single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with a bubble canopy and yellow tail accent, reminiscent of a WWII fighter. Features a streamlined fuselage, conventional tail, and taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, low-mounted straight wings, and yellow tail accent.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, bubble canopy, and the symmetrical wing layout.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with clear visibility of the wing and tail structure.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the taildragger landing gear, vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the narrow fuselage profile.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a bubble canopy.
